description_long: This barn features a tall, steeply pitched gable roof with a prominent central spire, giving it a striking vertical profile. The exterior is characterized by a half-timbered pattern, with dark brown wooden beams framing white infill panels, reminiscent of Tudor or medieval European architecture. The main entrance is set beneath a lower, secondary gable roof that projects outward, providing a sheltered entryway. Windows are small and symmetrically arranged, with a single window above the main door and additional windows on the side extensions. The structure appears robust and is likely constructed primarily from timber, with a spacious interior suitable for storage or agricultural use. The overall design suggests a blend of functional barn architecture with decorative, historical influences.
description: A tall, half-timbered barn with a steep gable roof and central spire, featuring a prominent entryway and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main entrance beneath a projecting gable roof and the tall central spire with half-timbered detailing.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the long, steeply pitched roof and the half-timbered pattern extending along the barn's length.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the vertical symmetry, central spire, and a single window above a secondary entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the extended roofline and the half-timbered pattern, with a small side extension visible.
attribute_roof_type: steeply pitched gable roof with central spire
attribute_door_style: single hinged main door beneath projecting gable
attribute_window_arrangement: single window above main door, additional small windows on side extensions
